title: Geometry, Scale, Similarity, and Spatial Planning · Model Lens: Volume Surface
summary: Create a scale plan for an accessible room layout with paths, clearances, paint, and flooring. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg volume surface increment in an accessible scale floor plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose volume or surface-area models for capacity, covering, and packaging tasks.
Choose and justify perimeter or area models for borders, surfaces, and composite regions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate Volume Surface evidence
steps.instruction: A rectangular working area measures 5 m by 3 m. Calculate its area.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Multiply perpendicular lengths and square the length unit: 5 m × 3 m.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Multiply perpendicular lengths and square the length unit: 5 m × 3 m.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes m² or m^2 or square meters.
